description_long: This sculpture depicts a standing anthropomorphic figure with the body of a human and the head of a falcon, adorned with a headdress featuring a large sun disk flanked by two horns. The figure is dressed in a traditional ancient Egyptian kilt and wears a broad collar and armlets, with detailed striations on the headdress and kilt. The arms are positioned with one arm bent forward and the other resting at the side, and the feet are placed firmly on a rectangular plinth decorated with hieroglyphic-like markings. The surface of the sculpture is a pale, stone-like color with subtle weathering and patina, giving it an aged appearance. The overall form is slender and upright, with careful attention to anatomical and ornamental details. The sculpture evokes the iconography of the Egyptian god Ra or Horus, commonly represented with a falcon head and sun disk.
description: A detailed, pale stone-like sculpture of an Egyptian falcon-headed deity with a sun disk headdress, standing on a decorated plinth. The figure is adorned with traditional garments and jewelry, featuring intricate surface details.
description_view_0: Frontal view showing the falcon-headed figure's face, sun disk headdress, and detailed chest ornamentation.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the profile of the falcon head, the sun disk, and the bent arm gesture.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the striated headdress, back of the kilt, and the plinth's rear surface.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the opposite profile, arm position, and the side of the plinth.
attribute_subject_matter: Egyptian falcon-headed deity with sun disk headdress
attribute_location_context: No specific installation context is visible; the sculpture appears isolated against a plain background.
